title: quercus pagoda var. leucophylla
intro: Quercus pagoda var. leucophylla, also known as the white-barked Chinese evergreen oak, is a deciduous tree species native to southern China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The extracts from Quercus pagoda var. leucophylla have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, offering benefits like reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and enhancing immune system function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and its leaves can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
